questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = \cos 2t$ and $y = \sin 2t$. Find $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ in terms of $t$.

steps.description: Differentiate x with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiate the trigonometric expression for $x$; remember $\dfrac{d}{dt}(\sin t) = \cos t$ and $\dfrac{d}{dt}(\cos t) = -\sin t$.

steps.description: Differentiate y with respect to t
steps.explanation: Differentiate $y$ using the same trigonometric rules.
Combine the derivatives using the parametric chain rule.
Put in the expressions for $\dot{x}$ and $\dot{y}$.
Simplify using identities such as $\tan t = \dfrac{\sin t}{\cos t}$ where appropriate.
The formula is valid only where $\dot{x} \neq 0$, so exclude values of $t$ that make the denominator zero.
This gives the slope of the tangent for admissible values of $t$.
